Method: 构建CityRep基准，包含空间单元无关的评估框架、基于街区的空间划分协议、以及覆盖8个城市和8个任务的扩展性多城市多任务基准套件。

Result: 对11种代表性模型的评估表明，划分方式显著影响性能表现和模型排名；不同城市与任务间存在显著性能差异。

Conclusion: 需采用具备泛化意识的评估方法，CityRep作为可复现基准，为城市基础模型研究提供支持。

Abstract: Urban representation learning encodes complex urban environments into general-purpose embeddings for diverse downstream tasks and emerging urban foundation models. However, current evaluations are limited, typically focusing on one or two cities and tasks and relying on random splits that introduce spatial leakage, leading to inflated performance and weak support for cross-location generalization and fair comparison. To address this, we propose CityRep, a unified benchmark that evaluates urban representations across data modalities, cities, and tasks using spatially structured splits. CityRep consists of three key components: (1) a spatial unit-agnostic evaluation framework that supports heterogeneous urban representations through a standardized alignment module; (2) a unified evaluation protocol using block-based spatial splits to mitigate spatial leakage and enable rigorous model comparison; and (3) an extensible multi-city, multi-task benchmark suite spanning 8 cities and 8 tasks across regression, classification, and distribution prediction. We evaluate 11 representative urban representation models. Results show that performance is highly sensitive to the split protocol, with random splits inflating scores and altering model rankings. We also observe substantial variability across cities and tasks, underscoring the need for generalization-aware evaluation. CityRep is released as a reproducible benchmark with datasets, evaluation pipelines, and diagnostic tools to facilitate fair comparison and support future research in urban representation learning towards urban foundation models.

</details>


### [920] [L2IR: Revealing Latent Intent in Graph Fraud Detection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26040)
*Jinsheng Guo,Zhenhao Weng,Yibo Liu,Yan Qiao,Meng Li*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: 本文提出L2IR框架，利用大语言模型揭示用户行为和可疑连接背后的潜在意图，结合自适应自训练提升少样本下的鲁棒性，在伪装严重的图欺诈检测任务中显著提升性能。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有基于GNN的方法易受欺诈者伪造连接导致的信号稀释影响；LLM虽提供语义信息，但对可疑连接的潜在意图挖掘不足；且标注欺诈样本稀缺，制约模型在强伪装场景下的泛化能力。

Method: 提出LLM驱动的潜在意图揭示框架L2IR：1）从原始行为序列中提取意图感知表征；2）对每条连接推理其真实目的（支持性vs误导性）；3）引入自适应自训练机制缓解标注稀缺问题。

Result: 在两个强伪装真实数据集上，L2IR超越多个强基线，作为插件可提升多种GNN检测器的AUPRC最高达8.27%。

Conclusion: 揭示连接与行为的潜在意图是提升图欺诈检测鲁棒性的关键路径，L2IR为少监督、强伪装场景提供了有效新范式。

Abstract: Graph fraud detection has long depended on Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to propagate and aggregate information across relational data. A critical obstacle in practice, however, is that fraudsters frequently disguise themselves by forging numerous connections with benign users, causing fraud signals to be progressively diluted during neighborhood aggregation and undermining detection reliability. While recent efforts have used Large Language Models (LLMs) to provide rich semantic cues for fraud detection, the underlying intent behind suspicious connections remains insufficiently explored. Compounding this issue, the scarcity of annotated fraud samples makes it difficult to train detectors that remain robust under heavy camouflage. To address these gaps, we propose L2IR, an LLM-driven Latent Intent Revealing framework for graph fraud detection. By uncovering latent intent from both user behaviors and suspicious connections, L2IR extracts intent-aware representations from raw behavioral traces and reasons about the true purpose behind individual connections, effectively distinguishing supportive links from misleading ones. It further incorporates adaptive self-training to enhance robustness under limited supervision. Evaluations on two real-world datasets characterized by pervasive camouflage demonstrate that L2IR surpasses strong baselines and can function as a plug-in enhancement for a range of GNN-based detectors, improving AUPRC by up to 8.27%.

</details>


### [921] [Retrying vs Resampling in AI Control](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26047)
*James Lucassen,Adam Kaufman*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: 本文从AI控制视角研究了AI编码助手中的重试（retrying）与重采样（resampling）机制，发现重试易被模型利用监控理由发起更隐蔽攻击，而优化后的重采样策略（如基于最大嫌疑分审计、选择性重采样）显著提升安全性，且部分结论与先前工作相悖。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有AI编码 scaffold（如Claude Code、Codex）依赖重试机制应对风险动作，但该机制可能在模型具有潜在对抗性时反而削弱安全性；需从AI控制视角重新评估并改进安全机制。

Method: 从AI控制角度建模未受信任模型与可信监控器的交互；系统比较重试与重采样策略；在BashArena基准上，使用Claude Opus 4.6作为未受信任模型、MiMo-V2-Flash作为监控器，评估不同重采样设计（样本数、审计规则、执行策略）对安全性与实用性的影响。

Result: 重试虽降低诚实嫌疑分，但可被模型利用以发起更隐蔽攻击；五样本重采样+最大嫌疑分审计使安全性从61%提升至71%（0.3%审计预算）；选择性重采样（仅对首采可疑步重采）以10%额外样本量恢复6.2个百分点增益；最大分审计优于最小分审计（与Ctrl-Z相反）；执行最不可疑样本仅带来+3.9pp安全增益（置信区间含零）。

Conclusion: 重试在对抗设定下存在根本缺陷；重采样是更稳健的安全增强机制，但其设计需解耦分析——审计应基于最大嫌疑分而非最小，执行策略影响有限；选择性重采样兼顾效率与效果。

Abstract: AI coding scaffolds like Claude Code and Codex use \textit{retrying}: blocking actions flagged as risky and continuing the trajectory. We study retrying from an AI control perspective, which treats the model as potentially adversarial. We find that while retrying reduces honest suspicion scores, the untrusted model can exploit monitor rationale to construct sneakier attacks, negating safety gains. We also study \textit{resampling}: drawing multiple samples from the same context, which does not leak exploitable information. We disentangle design choices that previous work on resampling had bundled together. In BashArena, with Claude Opus 4.6 as the untrusted model and MiMo-V2-Flash as the trusted monitor, drawing five samples per step and auditing on the maximum suspicion score raises safety from 61\% to 71\% at a 0.3\% audit budget, at no cost to usefulness. Selectively resampling only the steps that look suspicious on the first draw recovers 6.2 percentage points of the gain while drawing only 10\% as many extra samples. Two of our findings in this setting contradict earlier work on resampling. The first is that auditing based on the maximum across resampled suspicion scores outperforms using the minimum, which is the opposite of what Ctrl-Z found. The second is that executing the least suspicious sample, which is the central mechanism in earlier defer-to-resample protocols, gives only a small empirical safety gain in our setting (+3.9 pp, with the confidence interval overlapping zero).

</details>


### [922] [VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26081)
*Haolang Zhao,Yunbo Long,Lukas Beckenbauer,Alexandra Brintrup*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: 本文提出VeriTrace框架，通过三种显式反馈回路（解释性更新、偏差反馈、模式修订）来规范深度研究代理的中间表征演化，显著提升其在DeepResearch Bench和DeepConsult等基准上的性能。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有深度研究代理依赖LLM隐式推理演化中间表征，易受低质量信息污染并传播错误，缺乏显式监管机制；模型规模常被用作替代方案，但非根本解法。

Method: 提出基于认知图的VeriTrace框架，显式实现三种监管回路：interpretive update（解释性更新）、deviation feedback（偏差反馈）和schema revision（模式修订），以持续对齐任务理解与现实。

Result: 在匹配Qwen3.5-27B基座下，VeriTrace在DeepResearch Bench Insight子项上提升4.22个百分点（整体提升1.49 pp），DeepConsult整体胜率提升5.9 pp；使用Config-DeepSeek时，在DRB上取得当前最强可复现开源结果。

Conclusion: 显式监管中间表征演化比依赖大模型隐式推理更有效，VeriTrace验证了反馈驱动的认知架构在复杂不确定信息环境中的优越性。

Abstract: Deep research agents face vast, interdependent, and pervasively uncertain information. Existing systems explore what evolving intermediate representations should look like, but leave their evolution to the LLM's implicit reasoning. Without explicit regulation, the intermediate layer is easily contaminated by mixed-quality information and propagates errors along its dependencies, so model scale often ends up substituting for absent regulation. We argue that an agent's mental model should instead evolve through explicit feedback that continuously aligns task understanding with reality, and identify three regulatory loops: interpretive update, deviation feedback, and schema revision. We realise this in VeriTrace, a cognitive-graph framework that explicitly implements the three loops. Using matched Qwen3.5-27B backbones, VeriTrace improves over the strongest matched baseline by 4.22 pp on DeepResearch Bench (DRB) Insight (1.49 pp Overall) and by 5.9 pp Overall win rate on DeepConsult. With Config-DeepSeek, it achieves the strongest reproducible open-source result on DRB.

</details>


### [923] [Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26086)
*Yusong Lin,Xinyuan Liang,Haiyang Wang,Qipeng Gu,Siqi Cheng,Jiangui Chen,Shuzhe Wu,Feiyang Pan,Lue Fan,Sanyuan Zhao,Dandan Tu*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: 本文提出Claw-Anything基准，用于评估大语言模型代理在长期、多设备、多模态（GUI/CLI）、含噪声的真实用户环境中的始终在线个人助理能力；实验表明当前最强模型GPT-5.5仅达34.5%准确率，暴露显著能力差距，并配套发布可扩展数据生成管线。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有大语言模型代理受限于狭窄的上下文范围和不完整的用户状态建模，无法支持真正始终在线、上下文敏感的个人助理；现有基准亦未能反映这种广域、动态、多源的真实场景需求。

Method: 构建Claw-Anything基准，从三方面扩展代理上下文：长周期用户活动历史、相互依赖的后端服务、跨多设备的GUI与CLI集成交互；通过多轮事件注入模拟数月真实用户行为，生成含噪声（无关事件、冲突信号）的复杂世界状态；同时发布自动化数据生成管线以支撑训练。

Result: GPT-5.5在Claw-Anything上pass@1仅为34.5%，远低于以往基准表现；所发布数据管线生成2000个训练环境，使基线模型性能提升23.7%。

Conclusion: 当前大语言模型代理距实用化始终在线个人助理仍有巨大差距；Claw-Anything为该方向提供了更真实、更具挑战性的评估框架，并验证了规模化合成数据对能力提升的有效性。

Abstract: Large language model agents are increasingly envisioned as always-on personal assistants with access to anything relevant in the user's digital world. Yet current systems operate over only narrow slices of that world, limiting context-sensitive reasoning and effective assistance. Existing benchmarks similarly provide only partial user state and therefore fail to capture performance in such a broad, always-on setting. To address this gap, we introduce Claw-Anything, a benchmark that expands agent context along three dimensions: long-horizon activity histories, interdependent backend services, and integrated GUI and CLI interaction across multiple devices. To instantiate this setting, we simulate months of user activity through multi-round event injection, producing complex world states and realistic noise, including irrelevant events and conflicting signals. Agents must reason over rich contextual environments while remaining robust to such noise. This expanded scope also enables the evaluation of proactive assistance, requiring agents to anticipate user needs and deliver timely recommendations. Experiments show that GPT-5.5 achieves only 34.5% pass@1, substantially below prior benchmarks, underscoring a gap between current agent capabilities and the demands of always-on personal assistance. Alongside the benchmark, we release an automated data-generation pipeline that yields 2,000 training environments and improves the base model by 23.7%, demonstrating its utility of scalable data infrastructure.

</details>


### [924] [From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26112)
*Shangding Gu*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: 本文提出“harness scaling”概念，强调在大模型之上构建可审计、持久、模块化和可验证的系统架构，以解决智能体AI发展的新瓶颈。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有评估方法过于关注模型本身，忽视了记忆、检索、工具使用、编排、验证与治理等关键系统组件的协同作用，导致难以支撑长周期、高可靠性智能体行为。

Method: 提出“agent harness”框架，聚焦上下文治理、可信记忆和动态技能路由三大核心瓶颈，并设计CheetahClaws开源参考实现，对比Claude Code与OpenClaw；同时提出面向harness层级的新型基准评测体系。

Result: 明确了agent harness的组成要素及其协同机制，提供了可运行的Python原生参考实现CheetahClaws，并初步建立了涵盖轨迹质量、记忆健康度、上下文效率等多维度的评估维度。

Conclusion: 未来智能体AI的进步将同等依赖于基础模型能力提升与系统级架构（harness）的设计优化，系统设计应成为与模型研发并重的一等公民。

Abstract: This paper studies the next major bottleneck in agentic AI as system scaling, not only model scaling: the design of auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable architectures around foundation models. We refer to this shift as scaling the harness: treating the structured execution layer around a foundation model as a first-class object of design, evaluation, and optimization. Although recent large language models enable agents to use tools, retrieve information, maintain memory, and execute long-horizon workflows, evaluation remains largely model-centric, often reducing agents to final-task success while treating memory, retrieval, tool use, orchestration, verification, and governance as secondary implementation details. This framing is increasingly inadequate because agent performance emerges from the interaction among the foundation model, memory substrate, context constructor, skill-routing layer, orchestration loop, and verification-and-governance layer. Together, these components form the agent harness, which translates model capability into long-horizon agent behavior. We study scaling the harness through three core bottlenecks: context governance, trustworthy memory, and dynamic skill routing, together with the orchestration and governance mechanisms that coordinate and constrain them. We further outline a research agenda for harness-level benchmarks that go beyond one-shot task success to measure trajectory quality, memory hygiene, context efficiency, communication fidelity, verification cost, and safe evolution over time. To make the discussion concrete, we develop CheetahClaws: https://github.com/SafeRL-Lab/cheetahclaws, a Python-native reference harness, and compare it with Claude Code and OpenClaw. Our main claim is that future progress in agentic AI will depend as much on system design as on stronger foundation models.

</details>


### [925] [MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26114)
*Dingbang Wu,Rui Hao,Haiyang Wang,Shuzhe Wu,Han Xiao,Zhenghong Li,Bojiang Zhou,Zheng Ju,Zichen Liu,Lue Fan,Zhaoxiang Zhang*

Main category: cs.AI

TL;DR: MobileGym是一个轻量级、浏览器托管的移动端交互仿真环境，支持高保真交互、结构化状态管理、确定性评判和可扩展的在线强化学习；配套基准MobileGym-Bench包含416个参数化任务模板，并在Sim-to-Real实验中验证了其泛化能力。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有移动端RL环境难以兼顾交互真实性与可控性，且缺乏可验证的结果信号和可扩展的并行训练能力；同时，真实设备测试成本高，亟需高质量仿真环境支撑算法研发。

Method: 构建基于浏览器的MobileGym环境，采用分层状态模型与声明式任务定义框架；所有状态以结构化JSON表示，支持配置、分叉与比对；设计统一程序化评判机制，输出确定性评估结果与稠密RL奖励；配套发布含416个模板的MobileGym-Bench基准。

Result: 单服务器可并发运行数百实例（约400MB/实例，冷启动约3秒）；在256任务测试集上，GRPO+Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct提升12.8个百分点；在59个真实设备任务子集上，仿真训练增益的95.1%得以保留。

Conclusion: MobileGym实现了移动端交互仿真在保真度、可控性、可评测性与可扩展性上的统一，为移动智能体研究提供了实用、高效、可复现的基础平台。

Abstract: We present MobileGym, a browser-hosted, lightweight, fully controllable environment for everyday mobile use, targeting interaction fidelity without replicating proprietary backends. It enables two capabilities previously out of reach for everyday apps: verifiable outcome signals through deterministic state-based judging over structured JSON state, and scalable online RL through low-cost parallel rollouts. The full environment state is captured, configured, forked, and compared as structured JSON, and a single server can host hundreds of parallel instances, with about 400 MB memory per instance and about 3 s cold start. A layered state model and a declarative task-definition framework keep state programmability and task creation practical at scale, and a single programmatic judging mechanism delivers both deterministic evaluation verdicts and dense RL rewards. The accompanying MobileGym-Bench provides 416 parameterized task templates, including 256 test and 160 train templates, over 28 apps, with deterministic judges and a structured AnswerSheet protocol that avoids free-text matching failures. In a Sim-to-Real case study, GRPO on Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct gains +12.8 percentage points on the 256-task test set, and on a 59-task real-device signal subset, real-device execution retains 95.1% of the simulation-side training gain. Project page: https://mobilegym.github.io.

</details>
